title: Determination of bioavailable phosphorus in water samples using bioassay methods
author: Dijkstra, Auer, et al.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The use of a high-pressure filtration apparatus and filter scraping are likely to increase the fraction of bio P as algal cells in the sample may become damaged (aiding P-release). Measurements of the fraction of bio P may thus be seen as a conservative estimate. Bias in the method will not impact results when comparing bio P between systems or over time as they describe the relative difference.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Replication of results for the soluble and particulate phase assays is good. Assays performed on triplicate samples collected from the Fox River, WI and Lake Winnebago, WI yielded a coefficient of variation for TDP and SRP of 7.7 and 6.3%, respectively. Comparable results were obtained [11] in assays performed on samples collected from the Maumee River, OH with coefficients of variation of 6 and 5%, respectively.
